passages.title: Museum attendance and free evenings
passages.body:
Last year, the city’s art museum began offering free admission on Thursday evenings. Average Thursday evening attendance more than doubled compared with the previous year. Museum leaders announced that the free-evening policy had successfully expanded access to the arts.

Critics noted that the museum also launched a social-media campaign and installed a popular light installation on the same date the free evenings began. Thursday foot traffic in the surrounding neighbourhood rose overall, including at cafés that do not offer free admission.

passages.title: Breakfast study
passages.body: In a week-long diary study, teenagers who reported eating breakfast on most days were more likely than others to describe their mood as “steady” in the afternoon. The study’s press release claimed that eating breakfast stabilizes mood in adolescents. Participants self-reported both meals and mood; there was no clinical measure of blood sugar or sleep quality.

questions.prompt: Which point do the critics most directly use to challenge a simple “free admission caused higher attendance” story?
choices: Museum staff work longer hours on Thursdays.
Other changes and neighbourhood-wide traffic patterns coincided with the policy, so attendance could reflect more than free tickets alone
Doubled attendance still leaves the museum half-empty.
Light installations are usually displayed only in winter.
questions.answer: Other changes and neighbourhood-wide traffic patterns coincided with the policy, so attendance could reflect more than free tickets alone

questions.prompt: The museum leaders’ statement treats Thursday’s higher attendance chiefly as evidence of —
choices: wider public engagement tied to their pricing decision
a need to shorten visiting hours
a decline in interest during daytime hours
competition with nearby sports arenas
questions.answer: wider public engagement tied to their pricing decision

questions.prompt: The leap from the diary results to “breakfast stabilizes mood” is vulnerable mainly because —
choices: teenagers never eat lunch
diary studies always last exactly seven days
steady afternoon mood and breakfast habits might both stem from a third factor the study did not measure, such as sleep
mood cannot be described with adjectives
questions.answer: steady afternoon mood and breakfast habits might both stem from a third factor the study did not measure, such as sleep

questions.prompt: Which revision would most improve the strength of the breakfast–mood claim?
choices: Ask participants to guess how peers would rate their mood
Survey only students who skip breakfast
Publish the press release before collecting data
Replace self-reported mood with a standardized mood checklist and track sleep and wake times alongside meals
questions.answer: Replace self-reported mood with a standardized mood checklist and track sleep and wake times alongside meals

questions.prompt: Rule: A book may be labelled “Canadian spotlight” only if the author was born in Canada or the story is set mostly in Canada. A novel is set in Vancouver; the author was born in Ireland. May it carry the label?
choices: No, unless the author later became a citizen
No, because neither condition is met
Yes, but only in paperback
Yes, because Vancouver is in Canada
questions.answer: Yes, because Vancouver is in Canada

questions.prompt: Claim: “No one who cares about fairness would support this plan; Maya supports it, so Maya does not care about fairness.” This pattern of reasoning is structurally closest to —
choices: Everyone who studies passes; Jordan studies, so Jordan passes.
Only birds have feathers; this animal has feathers, so it is a bird.
No reptiles are mammals; this creature is a mammal, so it is not a reptile.
If it rains, the field is muddy; the field is muddy, so it rained.
questions.answer: No reptiles are mammals; this creature is a mammal, so it is not a reptile.

questions.prompt: Headline: “Cold snap linked to surge in online shopping.” The body explains that during three freezing weeks, parcel deliveries rose while in-store foot traffic fell. What should a careful reader conclude first?
choices: Cold weather necessarily makes people dislike stores.
Online shopping always causes cold weather.
Foot traffic never changes with the seasons.
Shopping moved channels during cold weather; the headline’s causal link may be oversimplified
questions.answer: Shopping moved channels during cold weather; the headline’s causal link may be oversimplified

questions.prompt: An advertisement shows a celebrity smiling while holding a water bottle. It does not claim the water improves health. The ad still tries to persuade mainly by —
choices: presenting statistical proof
defining scientific terms
listing ingredients in small print
transferring positive feelings from a liked figure to the product
questions.answer: transferring positive feelings from a liked figure to the product

questions.prompt: Lena argues the team should rehearse outdoors because fresh air helps concentration. Mateo replies that wind noise ruined last year’s outdoor run-through. Mateo’s response primarily —
choices: changes the subject to a different activity
proves that concentration is impossible outdoors
denies that fresh air exists
agrees with Lena’s reason but adds a practical drawback
questions.answer: agrees with Lena’s reason but adds a practical drawback

questions.prompt: Which sentence commits a false dichotomy?
choices: Many students use phones both for notes and for distraction.
Some teachers allow phones during research tasks.
Either we ban phones in class entirely or student learning will collapse.
Battery life varies by device model.
questions.answer: Either we ban phones in class entirely or student learning will collapse.
